name: Agent Authentication
description: Agent authentication is the process of verifying the identities of agents to ensure they are authorized and trustworthy participants within a system.

name: Application-based Process Isolation
description: Application code which prevents its own subroutines from accessing intra-process / internal memory space.

name: Active Certificate Analysis
description: Actively collecting PKI certificates by connecting to the server and downloading its server certificates for analysis.

name: Application Configuration Hardening
description: Modifying an application's configuration to reduce its attack surface.

name: Application Exception Monitoring
description: Monitoring the failures of system counters and timers.

name: Application Hardening
description: Application Hardening makes an executable application more resilient to a class of exploits which either introduce new code or execute unwanted existing code. These techniques may be applied at compile-time or on an application binary.

name: Asset Inventory
description: Asset inventorying identifies and records the organization's assets and enriches each inventory item with knowledge about their vulnerabilities.

name: Account Locking
description: The process of temporarily disabling user accounts on a system or domain.

name: Active Logical Link Mapping
description: Active logical link mapping sends and receives network traffic as a means to map the whole data link layer, where the links represent logical data flows rather than physical connection

name: Access Modeling
description: Access modeling captures and records the access permissions granted to identities (e.g., administrators, users, groups, systems) and optionally includes details on how these identities are stored, managed, and shared across systems.

name: Access Mediation
description: Access mediation is the process of granting or denying specific requests to: 1) obtain and use information and related information processing services; and 2) enter specific physical facilities (e.g., Federal buildings, military establishments, border crossing entrances). Access mediation decisions should enforce least privilege by granting access for scoped durations to prevent privilege creep and, where applicable, implement just-in-time (JIT) access. Denial decisions may prevent initial access or terminate access that has already been granted, ensuring continuous enforcement of security policies.

name: Administrative Network Activity Analysis
description: Detection of unauthorized use of administrative network protocols by analyzing network activity against a baseline.

name: Authentication Cache Invalidation
description: Removing tokens or credentials from an authentication cache to prevent further user associated account accesses.

name: Authentication Event Thresholding
description: Collecting authentication events, creating a baseline user profile, and determining whether authentication events are consistent with the baseline profile.

name: Access Policy Administration
description: Access policy administration is the systematic process of defining, implementing, and managing access control policies that dictate user permissions to resources.

name: Application Protocol Command Analysis
description: Analyzing application protocol level remote commands to detect unauthorized activity.

name: Active Physical Link Mapping
description: Active physical link mapping sends and receives network traffic as a means to map the physical layer.

name: Application Performance Monitoring
description: Monitoring the count and duration of the application or program cycle.

name: ARMA Model
description: Autoregressive-moving-average (ARMA) models provide a parsimonious description of a (weakly) stationary stochastic process in terms of two polynomials, one for the autoregression (AR) and the second for the moving average (MA).

name: Asset Vulnerability Enumeration
description: Asset vulnerability enumeration enriches inventory items with knowledge identifying their vulnerabilities.

name: Authorization Event Thresholding
description: Collecting authorization events, creating a baseline user profile, and determining whether authorization events are consistent with the baseline profile.

name: Bootloader Authentication
description: Cryptographically authenticating the bootloader software before system boot.

name: Biometric Authentication
description: Using biological measures in order to authenticate a user.

name: Broadcast Domain Isolation
description: Broadcast isolation restricts the number of computers a host can contact on their LAN.

name: Bus Message Authentication
description: Applies cryptographic primitives to individual bus frames to verify the sender's identity and ensure the integrity of the data payload.

name: Byte Sequence Emulation
description: Analyzing sequences of bytes and determining if they likely represent malicious shellcode.

name: Certificate Analysis
description: Analyzing Public Key Infrastructure certificates to detect if they have been misconfigured or spoofed using both network traffic, certificate fields and third-party logs.

name: Connection Attempt Analysis
description: Analyzing failed connections in a network to detect unauthorized activity.

name: Certificate-based Authentication
description: Requiring a digital certificate in order to authenticate a user.

name: Credential Compromise Scope Analysis
description: Determining which credentials may have been compromised by analyzing the user logon history of a particular system.

name: Change Default Password
description: Changing the default password means replacing the factory-set credentials with a strong, unique password before the device is deployed, preventing unauthorized access.

name: Credential Eviction
description: Credential Eviction techniques disable or remove compromised credentials from a computer network.

name: Certificate Rotation
description: Certificate rotation involves replacing digital certificates and their private keys to maintain cryptographic integrity and trust, mitigating key compromise risks and ensuring continuous secure communications.

name: Content Filtering
description: Content Filtering techniques aid in the process of analyzing an input file for malicious or erroneous content and outputing a sanitized version.

name: Content Format Conversion
description: Content format conversion is mechanical transformation from one format to another which may be normalization or specifically flattening.

name: Control Flow Integrity
description: Enforcing legal control flow transfers during application process execution.

name: Credential Hardening
description: Credential Hardening techniques modify system or network properties in order to protect system or network/domain credentials.

name: Connected Honeynet
description: A decoy service, system, or environment, that is connected to the enterprise network, and simulates or emulates certain functionality to the network, without exposing full access to a production system.

name: Configuration Inventory
description: Configuration inventory identifies and records the configuration of software and hardware and their components throughout the organization.

name: Container Image Analysis
description: Analyzing a Container Image with respect to a set of policies.

name: Content Modification
description: Modify content that does not comply with policy.

name: Content Excision
description: Removing specific, potentially malicious, parts of content

name: Content Rebuild
description: Rebuild the file according to the spec so any unreferenced components or objects are removed.

name: Content Substitution
description: Modifies specific digital content information by replacing it with something else.

name: Certificate Pinning
description: Persisting either a server's X.509 certificate or their public key and comparing that to server's presented identity to allow for greater client confidence in the remote server's identity for SSL connections.

name: Content Quarantine
description: Transfer content that does not comply with policy to a quarantine zone.

name: Credential Revocation
description: Deleting a set of credentials permanently to prevent them from being used to authenticate.

name: Credential Rotation
description: Credential rotation is a security procedure in which authentication credentials, such as passwords, API keys, or certificates, are regularly changed or replaced to minimize the risk of unauthorized access.

name: Credential Scrubbing
description: The systematic removal of hard-coded credentials from source code to prevent accidental exposure and unauthorized access.

name: Client-server Payload Profiling
description: Comparing client-server request and response payloads to a baseline profile to identify outliers.

name: Credential Transmission Scoping
description: Limiting the transmission of a credential to a scoped set of relying parties.

name: Content Validation
description: Verify and validate contents complies with policy

name: Dynamic Analysis
description: Executing or opening a file in a synthetic "sandbox" environment to determine if the file is a malicious program or if the file exploits another program such as a document reader.

name: Domain Account Monitoring
description: Monitoring the existence of or changes to Domain User Accounts.

name: Dead Code Elimination
description: Removing unreachable or "dead code" from compiled source code.

name: Decoy Environment
description: A Decoy Environment comprises hosts and networks for the purposes of deceiving an attacker.

name: Data Exchange Mapping
description: Data exchange mapping identifies and models the organization's intended design for the flows of the data types, formats, and volumes between systems at the application layer.

name: Disk Encryption
description: Encrypting a hard disk partition to prevent cleartext access to a file system.

name: Decoy File
description: A file created for the purposes of deceiving an adversary.

name: Data Inventory
description: Data inventorying identifies and records the schemas, formats, volumes, and locations of data stored and used on the organization's architecture.

name: Disk Erasure
description: Disk Erasure is the process of securely deleting all data on a disk to ensure that it cannot be recovered by any means.

name: Disk Formatting
description: Disk Formatting is the process of preparing a data storage device, such as a hard drive, solid-state drive, or USB flash drive, for initial use.

name: Disk Partitioning
description: Disk Partitioning is the process of dividing a disk into multiple distinct sections, known as partitions.

name: Driver Load Integrity Checking
description: Ensuring the integrity of drivers loaded during initialization of the operating system.

name: Domain Logic Validation
description: Validation of variable state in the context of the domain application.

name: Directional Network Link
description: Enforce one-way network communication by preventing two-way communication.

name: Decoy Network Resource
description: Deploying a network resource for the purposes of deceiving an adversary.

name: Domain Name Reputation Analysis
description: Analyzing the reputation of a domain name.

name: DNS Allowlisting
description: Permitting only approved domains and their subdomains to be resolved.

name: DNS Cache Eviction
description: Flushing DNS to clear any IP addresses or other DNS records from the cache.

name: DNS Denylisting
description: Blocking DNS Network Traffic based on criteria such as IP address, domain name, or DNS query type.

name: DNS Traffic Analysis
description: Analysis of domain name metadata, including name and DNS records, to determine whether the domain is likely to resolve to an undesirable host.

name: Decoy Object
description: A Decoy Object is created and deployed for the purposes of deceiving attackers.

name: Decoy Persona
description: Establishing a fake online identity to misdirect, deceive, and or interact with adversaries.

name: Direct Physical Link Mapping
description: Direct physical link mapping creates a physical link map by direct observation and recording of the physical network links.

name: Decoy Public Release
description: Issuing publicly released media to deceive adversaries.

name: Database Query String Analysis
description: Analyzing database queries to detect [SQL Injection](https://capec.mitre.org/data/definitions/66.html).

name: Disable Remote Access
description: Limiting access to a computing device which is not required through or from a non-organization-controlled network.

name: Domain Registration Takedown
description: The process of performing a takedown of the attacker's domain registration infrastructure.

name: Decoy Session Token
description: An authentication token created for the purposes of deceiving an adversary.

name: Domain Trust Policy
description: Restricting inter-domain trust by modifying domain configuration.

name: Decoy User Credential
description: A Credential created for the purpose of deceiving an adversary.

name: Executable Allowlisting
description: Using a digital signature to authenticate a file before opening.

name: Endpoint-based Web Server Access Mediation
description: Endpoint-based web server access mediation regulates web server access directly from user endpoints by implementing mechanisms such as client-side certificates and endpoint security software to authenticate devices and ensure compliant access.

name: Executable Denylisting
description: Blocking the execution of files on a host in accordance with defined application policy rules.

name: Email Filtering
description: Filtering incoming email traffic based on specific criteria.

name: Emulated File Analysis
description: Emulating instructions in a file looking for specific patterns.

name: Endpoint Health Beacon
description: Monitoring the security status of an endpoint by sending periodic messages with health status, where absence of a response may indicate that the endpoint has been compromised.

name: Exception Handler Pointer Validation
description: Validates that a referenced exception handler pointer is a valid exception handler.

name: Execution Isolation
description: Execution Isolation techniques prevent application processes from accessing non-essential system resources, such as memory, devices, or files.

name: Electronic Lock Monitoring
description: Monitoring electronic lock and door hardware states and access events (e.g., locked/unlocked, access granted/denied, door forced/held, tamper) to detect and respond to unauthorized entry.

name: Electromagnetic Radiation Hardening
description: The application of physical and material-level design measures to electronic systems, components, or facilities to reduce their susceptibility to damage or disruption from electromagnetic threats.

name: Physical Locking
description: Employ a mechanical locking device for securing moveable portions of physical barriers (e.g., doors, gates, drawers) in a secured position.

name: Email Removal
description: The email removal technique deletes email files from system storage.

name: Encrypted Tunnels
description: Encrypted encapsulation of routable network traffic.

name: File Analysis
description: File Analysis is an analytic process to determine a file's status. For example: virus, trojan, benign, malicious, trusted, unauthorized, sensitive, etc.

name: File Access Pattern Analysis
description: Analyzing the files accessed by a process to identify unauthorized activity.

name: Firmware Behavior Analysis
description: Analyzing the behavior of embedded code in firmware and looking for anomalous behavior and suspicious activity.

name: File Carving
description: Identifying and extracting files from network application protocols through the use of network stream reassembly software.

name: File Creation Analysis
description: Analyzing the properties of file create system call invocations.

name: File Content Decompression Checking
description: Checking if compressed or encoded data sections can be successfully decompressed or decoded. Can follow with further analysis with semantic knowledge

name: File Content Analysis
description: Employing a pattern matching algorithm to statically analyze the content of files.

name: File Content Rules
description: Employing a pattern matching rule language to analyze the content of files.

name: File Encryption
description: Encrypting a file using a cryptographic key.

name: Firmware Embedded Monitoring Code
description: Monitoring code is injected into firmware for integrity monitoring of firmware and firmware data.

name: File Eviction
description: File eviction techniques delete files from system storage.

name: File Format Verification
description: Verifying that a file conforms to its expected format specifications

name: File Hashing
description: Employing file hash comparisons to detect known malware.

name: File Hash Reputation Analysis
description: Analyzing the reputation of a file hash.

name: File Integrity Monitoring
description: Detecting any suspicious changes to files in a computer system.

name: File Internal Structure Verification
description: The process of checking specific static values within a file, such as file signatures or magic numbers, to ensure they match the expected values defined by the file format specification.

name: File Magic Byte Verification
description: Utilizing the magic number to verify the file

name: File Metadata Consistency Validation
description: The process of validating the consistency between a file's metadata and its actual content, ensuring that elements like declared lengths, pointers, and checksums accurately describe the file's content.

name: File Metadata Value Verification
description: The process of checking specific static values within a file, such as file signatures or magic numbers, to ensure they match the expected values defined by the file format specification.

name: Forward Resolution Domain Denylisting
description: Blocking a lookup based on the query's domain name value.

name: Forward Resolution IP Denylisting
description: Blocking a DNS lookup's answer's IP address value.

name: Firmware Verification
description: Cryptographically verifying firmware integrity.

name: Hardware-based Process Isolation
description: Preventing one process from writing to the memory space of another process through hardware based address manager implementations.

name: Hardware-based Write Protection
description: Physical methods of preventing data from being written to computer storage.

name: Hardware Component Inventory
description: Hardware component inventorying identifies and records the hardware items in the organization's architecture.

name: Homoglyph Detection
description: Comparing strings using a variety of techniques to determine if a deceptive or malicious string is being presented to a user.

name: Hierarchical Domain Denylisting
description: Blocking the resolution of any subdomain of a specified domain name.

name: Homoglyph Denylisting
description: Blocking DNS queries that are deceptively similar to legitimate domain names.

name: Host Reboot
description: Initiating a host's reboot sequence to terminate all running processes.

name: Host Shutdown
description: Initiating a host's shutdown sequence to terminate all running processes.

name: Identifier Activity Analysis
description: Taking known malicious identifiers and determining if they are present in a system.

name: Indirect Branch Call Analysis
description: Analyzing vendor specific branch call recording in order to detect ROP style attacks.

name: Identifier Analysis
description: Analyzing identifier artifacts such as IP address, domain names, or URL(I)s.

name: Input Device Analysis
description: Operating system level mechanisms to prevent abusive input device exploitation.

name: Integrated Honeynet
description: The practice of setting decoys in a production environment to entice interaction from attackers.

name: IO Port Restriction
description: Limiting access to computer input/output (IO) ports to restrict unauthorized devices.

name: IPC Traffic Analysis
description: Analyzing standard inter process communication (IPC) protocols to detect deviations from normal protocol activity.

name: IP Reputation Analysis
description: Analyzing the reputation of an IP address.

name: Identifier Reputation Analysis
description: Analyzing the reputation of an identifier.

name: Integer Range Validation
description: Ensuring that an integer is within a valid range.

name: Inbound Session Volume Analysis
description: Analyzing inbound network session or connection attempt volume.

name: Inbound Traffic Filtering
description: Restricting network traffic originating from untrusted networks destined towards a private host or enclave.

name: Job Function Access Pattern Analysis
description: Detecting anomalies in user access patterns by comparing user access activity to behavioral profiles that categorize users by role such as job title, function, department.

name: Kernel-based Process Isolation
description: Using kernel-level capabilities to isolate processes.

name: Local Account Monitoring
description: Analyzing local user accounts to detect unauthorized activity.

name: LAN Access Mediation
description: LAN access mediation encompasses the application of strict access control policies, systematic verification of devices, and authentication mechanisms to govern connectivity to a Local Area Network.

name: Local File Access Mediation
description: Local file access mediation is the process of an operating system granting or denying a specific access request to a local file.

name: Local File Permissions
description: Local file permissions is the systematic process of defining, implementing, and managing access control policies that dictate user permissions for accessing files on a local system through the configuration of operating system functionality.

name: Logical Link Mapping
description: Logical link mapping creates a model of existing or previous node-to-node connections using network-layer data or metadata.

name: Message Analysis
description: Analyzing email or instant message content to detect unauthorized activity.

name: Message Authentication
description: Authenticating the sender of a message and ensuring message integrity.

name: Memory Block Start Validation
description: Ensuring that a pointer accurately references the beginning of a designated memory block.

name: Memory Boundary Tracking
description: Analyzing a call stack for return addresses which point to unexpected  memory locations.

name: Message Encryption
description: Encrypting a message body using a cryptographic key.

name: Multi-factor Authentication
description: Requiring proof of two or more pieces of evidence in order to authenticate a user.

name: Message Hardening
description: The application of security controls to user-to-user and system-to-system communications so messages remain confidential, unaltered, and verifiable while resisting injection, replay, and tampering.

name: Motion Sensor Monitoring
description: Monitoring events from motion detectors (e.g., passive IR, microwave, dual-technology) to detect presence or movement within protected areas.

name: Network Access Mediation
description: Network access mediation is the control method for authorizing access to a system by a user (or a process acting on behalf of a user) communicating through a network, including a local area network, a wide area network, and the Internet.

name: Network Isolation
description: Network Isolation techniques prevent network hosts from accessing non-essential system network resources.

name: Network Mapping
description: Network mapping encompasses the techniques to identify and model the physical layer, network layer, and data exchange layers of the organization's network and their physical location, and determine allowed pathways through that network.

name: Network Node Inventory
description: Network node inventorying identifies and records all the network nodes (hosts, routers, switches, firewalls, etc.) in the organization's architecture.

name: Null Pointer Checking
description: Checking if a pointer is NULL.

name: Network Resource Access Mediation
description: Control of access to organizational systems and services by users or processes over a network.

name: Network Traffic Analysis
description: Analyzing intercepted or summarized computer network traffic to detect unauthorized activity.

name: Network Traffic Community Deviation
description: Establishing baseline communities of network hosts and identifying statistically divergent inter-community communication.

name: Network Traffic Filtering
description: Restricting network traffic originating from any location.

name: Network Traffic Policy Mapping
description: Network traffic policy mapping identifies and models the allowed pathways of data at the network, transport, and/or application levels.

name: Network Traffic Signature Analysis
description: Analyzing network traffic and compares it to known signatures

name: Network Vulnerability Assessment
description: Network vulnerability assessment relates all the vulnerabilities of a network's components in the context of their configuration and interdependencies and can also include assessing risk emerging from the network's design as a whole, not just the sum of individual network node or network segment vulnerabilities.

name: Operational Activity Mapping
description: Operational activity mapping identifies activities of the organization and the organization's suborganizations, groups, roles, and individuals that carry out the activities and then establishes the dependencies of the activities on the systems and people that perform those activities.

name: Operational Dependency Mapping
description: Operational dependency mapping identifies and models the dependencies of the organization's activities on each other and on the organization's performers (people, systems, and services.)  This may include modeling the higher- and lower-level activities of an organization forming a hierarchy, or layering, of the dependencies in an organization's activities.

name: Object Eviction
description: Terminate or remove an object from a host machine. This is the broadest class for object eviction.

name: Operational Logic Validation
description: Validation of variable state in the context of the control logic of the operational application.

name: Organization Mapping
description: Organization mapping identifies and models the people, roles, and groups with an organization and the relations between them.

name: Operating Mode Monitoring
description: Detects operating modes such as Program, Run, Remote, or Stop.

name: Operational Process Monitoring
description: Monitoring physical parameters and operator actions related to an operational environment.

name: Operating Mode Restriction
description: Restricting unauthorized changes to the operating mode prevents devices from switching into inappropriate or vulnerable states during normal use.

name: Operational Risk Assessment
description: Operational risk assessment identifies and models the vulnerabilities of, and risks to, an organization's activities individually and as a whole.

name: Operating System Monitoring
description: The operating system software, for D3FEND's purposes, includes the kernel and its process management functions, hardware drivers, initialization or boot logic. It also includes and other key system daemons and their configuration. The monitoring or analysis of these components for unauthorized activity constitute **Operating System Monitoring**.

name: Outbound Traffic Filtering
description: Restricting network traffic originating from a private host or enclave destined towards untrusted networks.

name: One-time Password
description: A one-time password is valid for only one user authentication.

name: OT Variable Access Restriction
description: Assign read/write access controls on designated registers or data tags to prevent unauthorized writes.

name: Process Analysis
description: Process Analysis consists of observing a running application process and analyzing it to watch for certain behaviors or conditions which may indicate adversary activity. Analysis can occur inside of the process or through a third-party monitoring application. Examples include monitoring system and privileged calls, monitoring process initiation chains, and memory boundary allocations.

name: Physical Access Mediation
description: Physical access mediation is the process of granting or denying specific requests to enter specific physical facilities (e.g., Federal buildings, military establishments, border crossing entrances.)

name: Pointer Authentication
description: Comparing the cryptographic hash or derivative of a pointer's value to an expected value.

name: Proxy-based Web Server Access Mediation
description: Proxy-based web server access mediation focuses on the regulation of web server access through intermediary proxy servers.

name: Passive Certificate Analysis
description: Collecting host certificates from network traffic or other passive sources like a certificate transparency log and analyzing them for unauthorized activity.

name: Process Code Segment Verification
description: Comparing the "text" or "code" memory segments to a source of truth.

name: Process Eviction
description: Process eviction techniques terminate or remove running process.

name: Physical Enclosure Hardening
description: Physical changes to a computer enclosure which reduce the ability for agents or the environment to affect the contained computer system.

name: Peripheral Firmware Verification
description: Cryptographically verifying peripheral firmware integrity.

name: Platform Hardening
description: Hardening components of a Platform with the intention of making them more difficult to exploit.  Platforms includes components such as: * BIOS UEFI Subsystems * Hardware security devices such as Trusted Platform Modules * Boot process logic or code * Kernel software components

name: Physical Access Monitoring
description: Monitoring the physical access of a specified environment through detection, recording, reviewing, and logging of who/what enters and exists areas.

name: Per Host Download-Upload Ratio Analysis
description: Detecting anomalies that indicate malicious activity by comparing the amount of data downloaded versus data uploaded by a host.

name: Process Lineage Analysis
description: Identification of suspicious processes executing on an end-point device by examining the ancestry and siblings of a process, and the associated metadata of each node on the tree, such as process execution, duration, and order relative to siblings and ancestors.

name: Passive Logical Link Mapping
description: Passive logical link mapping only listens to network traffic as a means to map the the whole data link layer, where the links represent logical data flows rather than physical connections.

name: Physical Link Mapping
description: Physical link mapping identifies and models the link connectivity of the network devices within a physical network.

name: Platform Monitoring
description: Monitoring platform components such as operating systems software, hardware devices, or firmware.

name: Protocol Metadata Anomaly Detection
description: Collecting network communication protocol metadata and identifying statistical outliers.

name: Password Rotation
description: Password rotation is a security policy that mandates the periodic change of user account passwords to mitigate the risk of unauthorized access due to compromised credentials.

name: Process Suspension
description: Suspending a running process on a computer system.

name: Process Spawn Analysis
description: Analyzing spawn arguments or attributes of a process to detect processes that are unauthorized.

name: Process Segment Execution Prevention
description: Preventing execution of any address in a memory region other than the code segment.

name: Proximity Sensor Monitoring
description: Monitoring events from proximity sensors that indicate a credential or tagged asset is within the sensor’s read range or a defined zone. Common enabling technologies include RFID, Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE), and Ultra-Wideband (UWB).

name: Process Self-Modification Detection
description: Detects processes that modify, change, or replace their own code at runtime.

name: Process Termination
description: Terminating a running application process on a computer system.

name: Platform Uptime Monitoring
description: Monitor the amount of time since the last power cycle or restart.

name: Pointer Validation
description: Ensuring that a pointer variable has the required properties for use.

name: Password Authentication
description: Password authentication is a security mechanism used to verify the identity of a user or entity attempting to access a system or resource by requiring the input of a secret string of characters, known as a password, that is associated with the user or entity.

name: Restore Access
description: Restoring an entity's access to resources.

name: Routing Access Mediation
description: Routing access mediation is a network security approach that manages and controls access at the network layer using VPNs, tunneling protocols, firewall rules, and traffic inspection to ensure secure and efficient data routing.

name: Resource Access Pattern Analysis
description: Analyzing the resources accessed by a user to identify unauthorized activity.

name: Restore Configuration
description: Restoring an software configuration.

name: Restore Database
description: Restoring the data in a database.

name: Restore Disk Image
description: Restoring a previously captured disk image a hard drive.

name: Restore Email
description: Restoring an email for an entity to access.

name: Restore File
description: Restoring a file for an entity to access.

name: Remote File Access Mediation
description: Remote file access mediation is the process of managing and securing access to file systems over a network to ensure that only authorized users or processes can interact with remote files.

name: RF Shielding
description: Adding physical barriers to a platform to prevent undesired radio interference.

name: Remote Firmware Update Monitoring
description: Monitoring of remote firmware update commands to identify unauthorized software installations.

name: Radiation Hardening
description: Radiation hardening is the process of making electronic components and circuits resistant to damage or malfunction caused by high levels of ionizing radiation.

name: Reissue Credential
description: Issue a new credential to a user which supercedes their old credential.

name: Registry Key Deletion
description: Delete a registry key.

name: Reference Nullification
description: Invalidating all pointers that reference a specific memory block, ensuring that the block cannot be accessed or modified after deallocation.

name: Restore Network Access
description: Restoring a entity's access to a computer network.

name: Restore Object
description: Restoring an object for an entity to access. This is the broadest class for object restoral.

name: Relay Pattern Analysis
description: The detection of an internal host relaying traffic between the internal network and the external network.

name: Reverse Resolution IP Denylisting
description: Blocking a reverse lookup based on the query's IP address value.

name: Restore Software
description: Restoring software to a host.

name: RPC Traffic Analysis
description: Monitoring the activity of remote procedure calls in communication traffic to establish standard protocol operations and potential attacker activities.

name: Remote Terminal Session Detection
description: Detection of an unauthorized remote live terminal console session by examining network traffic to a network host.

name: Restore User Account Access
description: Restoring a user account's access to resources.

name: Segment Address Offset Randomization
description: Randomizing the base (start) address of one or more segments of memory during the initialization of a process.

name: Service Binary Verification
description: Analyzing changes in service binary files by comparing to a source of truth.

name: System Call Analysis
description: Analyzing system calls to determine whether a process is exhibiting unauthorized behavior.

name: System Call Filtering
description: Controlling access to local computer system resources with kernel-level capabilities.

name: Source Code Hardening
description: Hardening source code with the intention of making it more difficult to exploit and less error prone.

name: System Configuration Permissions
description: Restricting system configuration modifications to a specific user or group of users.

name: Session Duration Analysis
description: Analyzing the duration of user sessions in order to detect unauthorized  activity.

name: System Daemon Monitoring
description: Tracking changes to the state or configuration of critical system level processes.

name: Script Execution Analysis
description: Analyzing the execution of a script to detect unauthorized user activity.

name: System File Analysis
description: Monitoring system files such as authentication databases, configuration files, system logs, and system executables for modification or tampering.

name: Stack Frame Canary Validation
description: Comparing a value stored in a stack frame with a known good value in order to prevent or detect a memory segment overwrite.

name: System Firmware Verification
description: Cryptographically verifying installed system firmware integrity.

name: Standalone Honeynet
description: An environment created for the purpose of attracting attackers and eliciting their behaviors that is not connected to any production enterprise systems.

name: System Init Config Analysis
description: Analysis of any system process startup configuration.

name: Scheduled Job Analysis
description: Analysis of source files, processes, destination files, or destination servers associated with a scheduled job to detect unauthorized use of job scheduling.

name: Sender MTA Reputation Analysis
description: Characterizing the reputation of mail transfer agents (MTA) to determine the security risk in emails.

name: Strong Password Policy
description: Modifying system configuration to increase password strength.

name: Sender Reputation Analysis
description: Ascertaining sender reputation based on information associated with a message (e.g. email/instant messaging).

name: Shadow Stack Comparisons
description: Comparing a call stack in system memory with a shadow call stack maintained by the processor to determine unauthorized shellcode activity.

name: Session Termination
description: Forcefully end all active sessions associated with compromised accounts or devices.

name: Software Update
description: Replacing old software on a computer system component.

name: Service Dependency Mapping
description: Service dependency mapping determines the services on which each given service relies.

name: Software Inventory
description: Software inventorying identifies and records the software items in the organization's architecture.

name: System Dependency Mapping
description: System dependency mapping identifies and models the dependencies of system components on each other to carry out their function.

name: System Mapping
description: System mapping encompasses the techniques to identify the organization's systems, how they are configured and decomposed into subsystems and components, how they are dependent on one another, and where they are physically located.

name: System Vulnerability Assessment
description: System vulnerability assessment relates all the vulnerabilities of a system's components in the context of their configuration and internal dependencies and can also include assessing risk emerging from the system's design as a whole, not just the sum of individual component vulnerabilities.

name: Transfer Agent Authentication
description: Validating that server components of a messaging infrastructure are authorized to send a particular message.

name: Token Binding
description: Token binding is a security mechanism used to enhance the protection of tokens, such as cookies or OAuth tokens, by binding them to a specific connection.

name: Token-based Authentication
description: Token-based authentication is an authentication protocol where users verify their identity in exchange for a unique access token. Users can then access the website, application, or resource for the life of the token without having to re-enter their credentials.

name: TPM Boot Integrity
description: Assuring the integrity of a platform by demonstrating that the boot process starts from a trusted combination of hardware and software and continues until the operating system has fully booted and applications are running.  Sometimes called Static Root of Trust Measurement (STRM).

name: Trusted Library
description: A trusted library is a collection of pre-verified and secure code modules or components that are used within software applications to perform specific functions. These libraries are considered reliable and have been vetted for security vulnerabilities, ensuring they do not introduce risks into the application.

name: URL Analysis
description: Determining if a URL is benign or malicious by analyzing the URL or its components.

name: User Account Permissions
description: Restricting a user account's access to resources.

name: User Behavior Analysis
description: User behavior analytics ("UBA") as defined by Gartner, is a cybersecurity process about detection of insider threats, targeted attacks, and financial fraud. UBA solutions look at patterns of human behavior, and then apply algorithms and statistical analysis to detect meaningful anomalies from those patterns-anomalies that indicate potential threats.' Instead of tracking devices or security events, UBA tracks a system's users. Big data platforms are increasing UBA functionality by allowing them to analyze petabytes worth of data to detect insider threats and advanced persistent threats.

name: User Data Transfer Analysis
description: Analyzing the amount of data transferred by a user.

name: User Geolocation Logon Pattern Analysis
description: Monitoring geolocation data of user logon attempts and comparing it to a baseline user behavior profile to identify anomalies in logon location.

name: Unlock Account
description: Restoring a user account's access to resources by unlocking a locked User Account.

name: URL Reputation Analysis
description: Analyzing the reputation of a URL.

name: User Session Init Config Analysis
description: Analyzing modifications to user session config files such as .bashrc or .bash_profile.

name: Variable Initialization
description: Setting variables to a known value before use.

name: Video Surveillance
description: Monitoring of physical areas via camera video feeds to deter, detect, and investigate unauthorized access and related security events.

name: Variable Type Validation
description: Ensuring that a variable has the correct type.

name: Web Session Activity Analysis
description: Monitoring changes in user web session behavior by comparing current web session activity to a baseline behavior profile or a catalog of predetermined malicious behavior.

name: Web Session Access Mediation
description: Web session access mediation secures user sessions in web applications by employing robust authentication and integrity validation, along with adaptive threat mitigation techniques, to ensure that access to web resources is authorized and protected from session-related attacks.

name: Average Absolute Deviation
description: The average absolute deviation (AAD) of a data set is the average of the absolute deviations from a central point.

name: ANN-based Clustering
description: Combines the principles of Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) and clustering methods.

name: Agglomerative Clustering
description: Agglomerative Clustering is a type of hierarchical clustering method where data points are grouped together based on similarity. Initially, each data point is treated as an individual cluster, and then in successive iterations, the closest clusters are merged until only one large cluster remains or until a specified stopping criterion is met.

name: Actor-Critic
description: Actor-Critic is a Temporal Difference(TD) version of Policy gradient. It has two networks: Actor and Critic. The actor decided which action should be taken and critic inform the actor how good was the action and how it should adjust. The learning of the actor is based on policy gradient approach. In comparison, critics evaluate the action produced by the actor by computing the value function.

name: Asymmetric Feature-based Transfer Learning
description: Homogeneous (where the metrics are the same for both source and target) asymmetric transformation mapping transforms the source feature space to align with that of the target or the target to that of the source. This, in effect, bridges the feature space gap and reduces the problem into a homogeneous transfer problem when further distribution differences need to be corrected.

name: Alethic Logic
description: Alethic logic is a modal logic that addresses the modalities of necessity and possibility.

name: Active Learning
description: Active learning aims to improve learning efficiency by allowing the learning algorithm to select which data to learn from.

name: ARIMA Model
description: An autoregressive integrated moving average (ARIMA) model is a generalization of an autoregressive moving average (ARMA) model.

name: Autoregressive Model
description: An autoregressive (AR) model is a representation of a type of random process; as such, it is used to describe certain time-varying processes in nature, economics, behavior, etc.

name: Association Rule Learning
description: Association rule learning is a rule-based machine learning method for discovering interesting relations between variables in large databases.

name: Adaptive Resonance Theory Clustering
description: Adaptive Resonance Theory (ART) Clustering is a  neural network algorithm used for clustering data and is open to new learning(i.e. adaptive) without discarding the previous or the old information(i.e. resonance).

name: Approximate String Matching
description: Approximate string matching is a form of string matching that allows errrors.

name: Answer Set Programming
description: Answer set programming is a form of declarative programming based on the stable model (answer set) semantics of logic programming.

name: Autoencoding
description: Autoencoders are specific type of deep learning architecture used for learning representation of data, typically for the purpose of dimensionality reduction. This is achieved by designing deep learning architecture that aims that copying input layer at its output layer.

name: Bootstrap Aggregating
description: Bootstrap aggregating, also called bagging (from bootstrap aggregating), is a machine learning ensemble meta-algorithm designed to improve the stability and accuracy of machine learning algorithms used in statistical classification and regression. It also reduces variance and helps to avoid overfitting. Although it is usually applied to decision tree methods, it can be used with any type of method. Bagging is a special case of the model averaging approach.

name: Bayesian Estimation
description: A Bayes estimator or a Bayes action is an estimator or decision rule that minimizes the posterior expected value of a loss function (i.e., the posterior expected loss).

name: Boolean Expression Matching
description: Boolean expression matching produces a Boolean truth value for a given boolean expression and assignment of values to variables in the expression.

name: BERT
description: Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) is based on a deep learning model in which every output element is connected to every input element, and the weightings between them are dynamically calculated based upon their connection.

name: Bayesian Hypothesis Testing
description: Bayesian hypothesis testing can be framed as a special case of model comparison where a model refers to a likelihood function and a prior distribution.

name: Bayesian Linear Regression
description: Bayesian linear regression is a type of conditional modeling in which the mean of one variable is described by a linear combination of other variables, with the goal of obtaining the posterior probability of the regression coefficients.

name: Bayesian Linear Regression Learning
description: A supervised learning method that builds a Bayesian linear regression model using training data.

name: Bayesian Method
description: Bayesian analysis is a statistical procedure which endeavors to estimate parameters of an underlying distribution based on the observed distribution.

name: Bayesian Model Averaging
description: A parameter estimate (or a prediction of new observations) obtained by averaging the estimates (or predictions) of the different models under consideration, each weighted by its model probability.

name: Bayesian Model Combination
description: Bayesian model combination (BMC) is an algorithmic correction to Bayesian model averaging (BMA). Instead of sampling each model in the ensemble individually, it samples from the space of possible ensembles (with model weights drawn randomly from a Dirichlet distribution having uniform parameters)

name: Bayes Optimal Classifier
description: A probabilistic model that makes the most probable prediction for a new example.

name: Bucket of Models
description: A "bucket of models" is an ensemble technique in which a model selection algorithm is used to choose the best model for each problem. When tested with only one problem, a bucket of models can produce no better results than the best model in the set, but when evaluated across many problems, it will typically produce much better results, on average, than any model in the set.

name: Boosting
description: Boosting is a sequential process where each subsequent model attempts to correct the errors of the previous model

name: C4.5
description: C4.5 is an algorithm that is strongly based off ID3. It creates decision trees the same way as ID3. C4.5 improves on several aspects of ID3, including handling discreet variables, handling training data with missing values, and has the ability to automatically prune the decision trees it creates.

name: C5.0
description: C5.0 is the next version of C4.5, which in turn is the upgrade from ID3. The only difference between C5.0 and C4.5 is some improvements made to C5.0.

name: Cluster Analysis
description: Cluster analysis or clustering is the task of grouping a set of objects in such a way that objects in the same group (called a cluster) are more similar (in some sense) to each other than to those in other groups (clusters).

name: CART
description: The CART algorithm is a type of classification algorithm that is required to build a decision tree on the basis of Gini’s impurity index.

name: Centroid-based Clustering
description: Centroid-based clustering organizes the data into non-hierarchical clusters, in contrast to hierarchical clustering defined below. K-means is the most widely-used centroid-based clustering algorithm. Centroid-based algorithms are efficient but sensitive to initial conditions and outliers.

name: Canopy Clustering
description: The canopy clustering algorithm is an unsupervised pre-clustering algorithm  often used as preprocessing step for the K-means algorithm or the Hierarchical clustering algorithm. It is intended to speed up clustering operations on large data sets.

name: Correlation Clustering
description: Correlation clustering provides a method for clustering a set of objects into the optimum number of clusters without specifying that number in advance.

name: Classification
description: Classification uses an algorithm to accurately assign test data into specific categories.

name: Convolutional Neural Network
description: A class of artificial neural network most commonly applied to analyze visual imagery.CNNs use a mathematical operation called convolution in place of general matrix multiplication in at least one of their layers.

name: Correlation
description: Correlation is the degree to which two or more quantities are linearly associated.

name: Coefficient of Variation
description: The coefficient of variation (CV), also known as relative standard deviation (RSD), is a standardized measure of dispersion of a probability distribution or frequency distribution.  The coefficient of variation (CV) is defined as the ratio of the standard deviation to the mean .

name: Central Tendency
description: A measure of central tendency ) is a summary measure that attempts to describe a whole set of data with a single value that represents the middle or centre of its distribution.

name: Cramer's V
description: Cramér's V (sometimes referred to as Cramér's phi and denoted as φc) is a measure of association between two nominal variables, giving a value between 0 and +1 (inclusive) and is based on Pearson's chi-squared statistic.

name: CycleGAN
description: The Cycle Generative Adversarial Network (CycleGAN) is an approach to training a deep convolutional neural network for image-to-image translation tasks by mapping between input and output images using unpaired dataset.

name: Discriminant Analysis
description: Discriminant analysis attempts to establish whether a set of variables can be used to distinguish between two or more groups of cases.

name: Datalog
description: Datalog is a declarative logic programming language that is a syntactically a subset of Prolog.

name: DNN-based Clustering
description: DNNs serve for clustering as mappings to better representations. The features of these representations can be drawn from different layers of the network or even from several layers.

name: Density-based Clustering
description: Density-based clustering connects areas of high example density into clusters. This allows for arbitrary-shaped distributions as long as dense areas can be connected.

name: Distribution-based Clustering
description: Distribution-based clustering creates and groups data points based on their likely hood of belonging to the same probability distribution (Gaussian, Binomial, etc.) in the data.

name: DBSCAN
description: A density-based clustering algorithm that works on the assumption that clusters are dense regions in space separated by regions of lower density.

name: Divisive Clustering
description: A divisive clustering approach is a hierarchical, top-down approach to clustering a dataset.

name: Deep Convolutional GAN
description: Deep Convolutional GAN (DCGAN) uses convolutional and convolutional-transpose layers in the generator and discriminator, respectively.

name: Deontic Logic
description: Deontic logic addresses the modality of obligations and norms; i.e., the modality of morality.

name: Description Logic
description: A description logic (DL) is a form of logic usually more expressive than propositional logic but less expressive than first-order logic.

name: Deep Neural Network Classification
description: A deep neural network (DNN) is an artificial neural network (ANN) with multiple layers between the input and output layers. There are different types of neural networks but they always consist of the same components: neurons, synapses, weights, biases, and functions. These components as a whole function similarly to a human brain, and can be trained like any other ML algorithm

name: Distribution Properties
description: The properties derived from a probability distribution.

name: Dyna-Q
description: A Dyna-Q agent combines acting, learning, and planning.

name: Deep Q-learning
description: Uses a deep convolutional neural network, with layers of tiled convolutional filters to mimic the effects of receptive fields.

name: Dimension Reduction
description: Dimensionality reduction is a key technique within unsupervised learning. It compresses the data by finding a smaller, different set of variables that capture what matters most in the original features, while minimizing the loss of information.

name: Descriptive Statistics
description: Descriptive statistics provide simple summaries about the sample and about the observations that have been made.

name: Decision Tree
description: Decision tree learning is a supervised learning approach used in statistics, data mining, and machine learning. In this formalism, a classification or regression decision tree is used as a predictive model to draw conclusions about a set of observations.

name: Decision Tree Regression
description: Decision Trees Regression is asupervised learning method with the goal  to create a model that predicts the value of a target variable by learning simple decision rules inferred from the data features

name: Density-weighted Method
description: An Actvie Learning technique that uses a density estimate meta-parameter to avoid sampling sparsely populated regions of the feature space and can be based parametrically or from a parameter free model.

name: Expected Error Reduction
description: Expected Error Reduction (EER) follows similar ideas as EMC, but again looks at the model output instead of the model itself and also takes the other data into account. In particular, a sample x is considered useful, if we can expect that knowing the label will reduce the future error on unseen samples

name: Epistemic Logic
description: Epistemic logic addresses modalities of knowledge; i.e., the certainty of sentences.

name: Ensemble Learning
description: In statistics and machine learning, ensemble methods use multiple learning algorithms to obtain better predictive performance than could be obtained from any of the constituent learning algorithms alone

name: Exact Matching
description: Exact matching for numeric types is just the simple test for mathematical equivalence of the values being matched.

name: Equivalence Matching
description: Equivalence matching is matching two values, which may be bound to variables, to see if they are equivalent.

name: Expected Model Change
description: Supervised learning establishes a relationship between the known input and output variables to conduct a predictive analysis.

name: Expectation-maximization Clustering
description: An unsupervised clustering algorithm and extends to NLP applications like Latent Dirichlet Allocation, the Baum-Welch algorithm for Hidden Markov Models, and medical imaging.

name: Estimation
description: Estimation represents ways or a process of learning and determining the population parameter based on the model fitted to the data.

name: Fuzzy Logic
description: Fuzzy logic is a form of many-valued logic in which the truth value of variables may be any real number between 0 and 1.

name: First-order Logic
description: First-order logic is a collection of formal systems used in mathematics, philosophy, linguistics, and computer science. First-order logic uses quantified variables over non-logical objects, and allows the use of sentences that contain variables.

name: Goodman and Kruskal's Gamma
description: Goodman-Kruskal $\\gamma$ is a measure of rank correlation between x and y and is given by $(n_c -n_d) / (n_c + n_d)$, where $n_c$ is the number of concordant pairs of the observations and $n_d$ is the number of discordant pairs.

name: Generative Adversarial Network
description: Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN) are an approach to generative modeling using deep learning methods, such as convolutional neural networks.

name: Graph-based Clustering
description: Graph-based Clustering is a form of clustering where data is represented with graphs to identify clusters.  We include Connection-based Clustering in this class.

name: Grid-based Clustering
description: Divides the entire data space into a finite number of cells reducing the complexity of the data and focuses on the cells rather than the data.

name: Gradient-Boosted Decision Tree
description: A gradient-boosted decision tree is, as in other bagging and boosting methods, a method where the relatively 'weak' machine learning model (a decision tree) is used in an ensemble to form a 'strong' machine learning model.

name: Graph-based Semi-supervised Learning
description: Graph-based Semi-Supervised Learning (GSSL) methods aim to classify unlabeled data by learning the graph structure and labeled data jointly.

name: Grid-CNN
description: A class of neural networks that specializes in processing data that has a grid-like topology, such as an image.

name: Geometric Mean
description: The nth root of the product of the data values, where there are n of these. This measure is valid only for data that are measured absolutely on a strictly positive scale.

name: GPT
description: Generative pre-trained transformers (GPT) are a type of large language model (LLM) and a prominent framework for generative artificial intelligence.

name: Gated Recurrent Unit
description: The GRU is like a long short-term memory (LSTM) with a forget gate, but has fewer parameters than LSTM, as it lacks an output gate. GRU's performance on certain tasks of polyphonic music modeling, speech signal modeling and natural language processing was found to be similar to that of LSTM

name: Heterogeneous Asymmetric Feature-based Transfer Learning
description: Asymmetric transformation mapping  transforms the source feature space to align with that of the target or the target to that of the source. This, in effect, bridges the feature space gap and reduces the problem into a homogeneous transfer problem when further distribution differences need to be corrected.

name: Hybrid-based Transfer Learning
description: This method creates an asymmetric mapping from the target to the source and takes into account bias issues of cross-domain correspondences.

name: Hierarchical Clustering
description: Hierarchical clustering (also called hierarchical cluster analysis or HCA) is a method of cluster analysis that seeks to build a hierarchy of clusters.

name: High-dimension Clustering
description: The cluster analysis of data with anywhere from a few dozen to many thousands of dimensions.

name: Heterogeneous Feature-based Transfer Learning
description: Symmetric transformation  takes both the source feature space Xs and target feature space Xt and learns feature transformations as to project each onto a common subspace Xc for adaptation purposes. This derived subspace becomes a domain-invariant feature subspace to associate cross-domain data, and in effect, reduces marginal distribution differences.

name: Harmonic Mean
description: The reciprocal of the arithmetic mean of the reciprocals of the data values. This measure too is valid only for data that are measured absolutely on a strictly positive scale.

name: Higher-order Logic
description: Higher-order logic is a form of predicate logic that is distinguished from first-order logic by additional quantifiers and, sometimes, stronger semantics. Higher-order logics with their standard semantics are more expressive, but their model-theoretic properties are less well-behaved than those of first-order logic.

name: Hypothesis Testing
description: A statistical hypothesis test is a method of statistical inference used to decide whether the data at hand sufficiently support a particular hypothesis. Hypothesis testing allows us to make probabilistic statements about population parameters.

name: Heterogeneous Transfer Learning
description: Heterogeneous transfer learning is characterized by the source and target domains having differing feature spaces, but may also be combined with other issues such as differing data distributions and label spaces.

name: Homogenous Transfer Learning
description: In homogeneous transfer learning, the feature spaces of the source and target domains is of the same dimension (Ds = Dt) while the data of both domains is represented by the same attributes (Xs = Xt) and labels (Ys = Yt). Thus, homogeneous transfer learning aims to bridge the gap in the data distributions experienced during cross-domain transfer.

name: Instance-based Transfer Learning
description: Instance-based transfer learning methods try to reweight the samples in the source domain in an attempt to correct for marginal distribution differences. These reweighted instances are then directly used in the target domain for training.

name: ID3
description: ID3 stands for Iterative Dichotomiser 3 and is named such because the algorithm iteratively (repeatedly) dichotomizes(divides) features into two or more groups at each step.

name: Interval Estimation
description: Interval estimation is the use of sample data to estimate an interval of possible values of a parameter of interest.

name: Interquartile Range
description: The interquartile range (IQR) is a measure of statistical dispersion, which is the spread of the data and is defined as the difference between the 75th and 25th percentiles of the data.

name: Inferential Statistics
description: Statistical inference is the process of using data analysis to infer properties of an underlying distribution of probability.

name: Image Synthesis GAN
description: Image synthesis thorugh the application of Generative Adversarial Networks.

name: Intrinsically Semi-supervised Learning
description: These methods directly optimize an objective function with components for labeled and unlabeled samples and do not rely on any intermediate steps or supervised base learners. Basically, these methods are extension of existing supervised methods to include the effect of unlabeled data samples in the objective function.

name: Image-to-Image Translation GAN
description: Image-to-image translation is the task of transferring styles and characteristics from one image domain to another.

name: K-Center Clustering
description: K-center Clustering is a type of clustering based on an combinatorial optimization methods.  It clusters a set of points so as to minimize the maximum intercluster distance.

name: K-Fold Cross-Validation
description: Cross-validation is a resampling procedure used to evaluate machine learning models on a limited data sample. The procedure has a single parameter called k that refers to the number of groups that a given data sample is to be split into. As such, the procedure is often called k-fold cross-validation. When a specific value for k is chosen, it may be used in place of k in the reference to the model, such as k=10 becoming 10-fold cross-validation

name: K-means Clustering
description: K-means algorithm identifies k number of centroids, and then allocates every data point to the nearest cluster, while keeping the centroids as small as possible.

name: K-Nearest Neighbors
description: The k-nearest neighbors algorithm, also known as KNN or k-NN, is a non-parametric, supervised learning classifier, which uses proximity to make classifications or predictions about the grouping of an individual data point.

name: Kendall's Rank Correlation Coefficient
description: Kendall's $\\tau$ between and is given by $(n_c - n_d) / \\sqrt((n_c+n_d+n_x)(n_c+n_d+n_y)$, where is the number of concordant pairs of observations, is the number of discordant pairs, is the number of ties involving only the variable, and is the number of ties involving only the variable." ;

name: Kurtosis
description: The measure of the "fatness" of the tails of a pmf or pdf. The fourth standardized moment of the distribution.

name: Linear Classifier
description: A linear classifier is a model that makes a decision to categories a set of data points to a discrete class based on a linear combination of its explanatory variables

name: Linear Logic Programming
description: Linear logic programming is a form of logic programming that uses linear logic, that is, it emphasizes the use of formulas as resources.

name: Levenschtein Matching
description: The Levenshtein distance (LD) is a metric for measuring the differences between two sequences - or strings. Informally, the LD is the number of individual edits one would have to make to turn one sequence into another.

name: Logic Programming
description: Logic programming is a programming paradigm which is largely based on formal logic.

name: Linear Regression
description: Statistical regression method used for predictive analysis by modeling the linear relationship between independent and dependent variables.

name: Logistic Regression
description: Logistic regression is estimating the parameters of a logistic model.

name: Logical Rules
description: A logical rule matches event data or set of values to a conditional expression and results in the determination of a truth value, which may be used to determine the next action or step to take.

name: Logistic Regression Learning
description: A supervised learning method that builds a logistic regression model using training data.

name: Linear Regression Learning
description: A supervised learning method that builds a linear regression model using training data.

name: Long Short-term Memory
description: Unlike standard feedforward neural networks, LSTM has feedback connections. Such a recurrent neural network (RNN) can process not only single data points (such as images), but also entire sequences of data (such as speech or video). This characteristic makes LSTM networks ideal for processing and predicting data

name: Multivariate Analysis
description: Multivariate statistics encompassed the simultaneous observation and analysis of more than one outcome variable, i.e., multivariate random variables.

name: Median Absolute Deviation
description: The median absolute deviation (also MAD) is the median of the absolute deviation from the median.

name: Mean Absolute Deviation
description: The mean absolute deviation (MAD), also referred to as the "mean deviation" or sometimes "average absolute deviation", is the mean of the data's absolute deviations around the data's mean: the average (absolute) distance from the mean.

name: Moving Average Model
description: the moving-average model (MA model) is an approach for modeling univariate time series and specifies that the output variable is cross-correlated with a non-identical to itself random-variable.

name: Model-based Policy Optimization
description: Model-based policy optimization (MBPO) is a model-based, online, off-policy reinforcement learning algorithm. For more information on the different types of reinforcement learning agents

name: Model-based Reinforcement Learning
description: Model-based Reinforcement Learning refers to learning optimal behavior indirectly by learning a model of the environment by taking actions and observing the outcomes that include the next state and the immediate reward. The models predict the outcomes of actions and are used in lieu of or in addition to interaction with the environment to learn optimal policies.

name: Model-based Value Iteration
description: Value Iteration effectively reducesthe evaluation stage down to a single sweep of the states. Additionally, to improve things further, it combines the Policy Evaluation and Policy Improvement stages into a single update.

name: Mean
description: The sum of all measurements divided by the number of observations in the data set.

name: Median
description: The middle value that separates the higher half from the lower half of the data set. The median and the mode are the only measures of central tendency that can be used for ordinal data, in which values are ranked relative to each other but are not measured absolutely.

name: Model-free Reinforcement Learning
description: In reinforcement learning (RL), a model-free algorithm (as opposed to a model-based one) is an algorithm which does not use the transition probability distribution (and the reward function) associated with the Markov decision process (MDP),which, in RL, represents the problem to be solved. The transition probability distribution (or transition model) and the reward function are often collectively called the "model" of the environment (or MDP), hence the name "model-free". A model-free RL algorithm can be thought of as an "explicit" trial-and-error algorithm. An example of a model-free algorithm is Q-learning.

name: Machine Learning
description: Machine learning techniques are computational methods that combine statistics, probability, and optimization to make accurate predictions and/or improve performance.

name: Modal Logic
description: Modal logic is a collection of formal systems developed to represent statements about necessity and possibility. It plays a major role in philosophy of language, epistemology, metaphysics, and natural language semantics.

name: Maximum-margin Learning
description: Maximum-margin classifiers attempt to maximize the distance between the given data points and the decision boundary

name: Mode
description: The most frequent value in the data set. This is the only central tendency measure that can be used with nominal data, which have purely qualitative category assignments.

name: Moments
description: With a probability distribution function, the the first moment is the expected value, the second central moment is the variance, the third standardized moment is the skewness, and the fourth standardized moment is the kurtosis.

name: Multilayer Perceptron Classification
description: A multilayer perceptron (MLP) is a fully connected class of feedforward artificial neural network (ANN).An MLP consists of at least three layers of nodes: an input layer, a hidden layer and an output layer.

name: Multiple Regression
description: Multiple (linear) regression attempts to model the relationship between two or more explanatory variables and a response variable by fitting a linear equation to observed data.

name: Multiple Regression Learning
description: A supervised learning method that builds a multiple regression model using training data.

name: Naive Bayes Classifier
description: The Naïve Bayes classifier is a supervised machine learning algorithm, which is used for classification tasks, like text classification. It is also part of a family of generative learning algorithms, meaning that it seeks to model the distribution of inputs of a given class or category.

name: Non-monotonic Logic
description: Non-monotonic logic is a formal logic whose conclusion relation is not monotonic. In other words, non-monotonic logics are devised to capture and represent defeasible inferences (cf. defeasible reasoning), i.e., a kind of inference in which reasoners draw tentative conclusions, enabling reasoners to retract their conclusion(s) based on further evidence.

name: Numeric Pattern Matching
description: Numeric pattern matching uses a pattern specification and sees if the numeric value matches that pattern--simple forms include exact matching and range matching.

name: Non-Parametric Tests
description: A non-parametric test relies is used when the underlying distribution of data is non-symmetric (non-normal distribution).

name: Nonlinear Regression
description: Nonlinear regression is a form of regression analysis in which observational data are modeled by a function which is a nonlinear combination of the model parameters and depends on one or more independent variables.

name: Nonlinear Regression Learning
description: A supervised learning method that builds a non-linear regression model using training data.

name: OWL
description: The Web Ontology Language (OWL) is a family of knowledge representation languages for authoring ontologies.

name: Projection-based Clustering
description: Projection Based Clustering is a clustering framework based on a chosen projection method and projection method a parameter-free high-dimensional data visualization technique.

name: Point-biserial Correlation Coefficient
description: The point biserial correlation coefficient (rpb) is a correlation coefficient used when one variable (e.g. Y) is dichotomous; Y can either be "naturally" dichotomous, like whether a coin lands heads or tails, or an artificially dichotomized variable.

name: Perturbation-based Learning
description: Perturbation based methods are proposed under the smoothness assumption, which indicates that two data points close to each other in feature space are likely to have the same label.

name: Parameter-based Transfer Learning
description: The idea behind parameter-based methods is that a well-trained model on the source domain has learned a well-defined structure, and if two tasks are related, this structure can be transferred to the target model.

name: Phi Coefficient
description: The phi coefficient (or mean square contingency coefficient is a measure of association for two binary variables.

name: Projected Clustering
description: Projected clustering is a dimension reduction subspace clustering method.

name: Principal Components Analysis
description: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is a statistic-based method of identifying patterns in a large dataset while increasing interpretability and preserving information.

name: Principal Component Analysis
description: Principal components analysis (PCA) creates a new set of orthogonal variables that contain the same information as the original set. It rotates the axes of variation to give a new set of orthogonal axes, ordered so that they summarize decreasing proportions of the variation.

name: Point Estimation
description: A point estimation is a single value that estimates the parameter. Point estimates are single values calculated from the sample

name: Policy Gradient
description: The objective of a Reinforcement Learning Policy Gradient agent is to maximize the “expected” reward when following a policy

name: Progressively Growing GAN
description: Progressive Growing GAN (ProGAN) is an extension to the GAN training process that allows for the stable training of generator models that can output large high-quality images.

name: Pix2Pix
description: Pix2Pix is based on condtional GAN architecture and are trained on paired set of images or scenes from two domains to be used for translation.

name: Probabilistic Logic
description: Probabilistic logic extends traditional logic truth tables with probabilistic expressions.

name: Propositional Logic
description: Propositional logic deals with statements (i.e., propositions, which can be true or false) and relations between propositions, including the construction of arguments based on them.

name: Predicate Logic
description: Predicate logic is is collection of formal systems used in mathematics, philosophy, linguistics, and computer science. First-order logic and Higher-order logic both incorporate predicate logic.

name: Pattern Matching
description: Pattern matching is the act of checking a given sequence of tokens for the presence of the constituents of some pattern.

name: Partial Matching
description: Partial string pattern matching is a special case of string pattern matching where one seeks to find a pattern within a larger string (text). It allows for the detection of patterns that occur as substrings or partial segments within the full string, rather than requiring an exact match across the entire string.

name: Prolog
description: Prolog has its roots in first-order logic, a formal logic, and unlike many other programming languages.

name: Parametric Tests
